look.text: The coupler sits snug in the socket, colony net linked. A green light ticks in waltz time.
A bare comm socket under the beacon head — the station's line to the colony net, sixty years unplugged.

use.text: Linked and humming. Leave it be.
Nothing to connect it WITH. It wants a coupler.

itemUse.text: Cog splices the coupler in with surgical calm. "Colony net: reachable. Volume control: I have taken a liberty."
Sixty-year-old pinouts. You want an android's steady hands for this — good thing you brought one.
The sniffer chirps: socket live, line dormant, and — it adds, unprompted — the song is in D minor.
Power it has, a voice it lacks — the socket wants a coupler, not a battery. Slotting the cell here would light a lamp nobody's asking about.

look.text: Behind this grille, sixty years of patience and one caretaker intelligence, singing.

itemUse.text: The sniffer presses to the grille and hesitates. Readout: emotional state — UNRECOGNISED. It files the result, uncertainly, under 'maintenance'.

look.text: Through the salt-scoured glass: the aurora, banding green to violet, and dark shapes riding it. Sky-rays. They're circling the beacon.

use.text: It hasn't opened since before you were born, and it's not starting now.

exits.name: landing bay door
exits.look: Back down to the landing bay.
